children.heading: Establishment of National Supply Chain Database
children.label: a Establishment of National Supply Chain Database
children.text: The Director shall establish a voluntary National Supply Chain Database, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: b Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the voluntary National Supply Chain Database shall be to assist the Federal Government and industry sectors in minimizing disruptions to the United States supply chain by having an assessment of United States manufacturers’ capabilities.

children.heading: Study on National Supply Chain Database
children.label: c Study on National Supply Chain Database
children.text: In establishing the National Supply Chain Database, the Director shall consider the findings and recommendations from the study authorized pursuant to section 9413 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021 (), including measures to secure and protect the Database from adversarial attacks and vulnerabilities.Public Law 116–283

children.heading: Database and Manufacturing Extension Partnership
children.label: d Database and Manufacturing Extension Partnership

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall establish the infrastructure for the National Supply Chain Database through the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership, established pursuant to , by connecting information from the Centers (as such term is defined in such section) through the Database.section 278k of title 15

children.label: A
children.text: provide a national overview of the networks of supply chains of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: support understanding of whether there is a need for some manufacturers to retool in some critical areas to meet the urgent need for key products.

children.heading: Individual Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership Center databases
children.label: 3 Individual Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership Center databases
i
children.text: each Center is connected to the National Supply Chain Database; and

children.label: ii
children.text: each supply chain database maintained by a Center is interoperable with the Database.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to require a State or territory of the United States to establish a new supply chain database through the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership program.

children.heading: Maintenance of National Supply Chain Database
children.label: e Maintenance of National Supply Chain Database

children.text: shall maintain the National Supply Chain Database as an integration of State-level databases from the Center of each State or territory of the United States;
may populate the Database with information from past or current clients of Centers; and
may include in the Database information voluntarily provided by non-client private sector entities based and operating in the United States, as applicable and appropriate.
Basic private sector entity information.
An overview of capabilities, accreditations, and products.
Proprietary information.
Sector 31-33 – Manufacturing.
Sector 54 – Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services.
Sector 48-49 – Transportation and Warehousing.
Level 1 shall have the capability to provide basic private sector entity information and shall be available to the public.
Level 2 shall have the capability to provide a deeper, nonproprietary overview into capabilities, products, and accreditations and shall be available to all companies that contribute to the Database.
Level 3 shall have the capability to hold proprietary information.

children.heading: Matters relating to disclosure and access
children.label: i Matters relating to disclosure and access

children.heading: FOIA exemption
children.label: 1 FOIA exemption
children.text: The National Supply Chain Database, and any information contained therein that is not publicly released by the Institute, shall be exempt from public disclosure under .section 552(b)(3) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: the contributing private sector entity, the Institute, and staff from a Center who sign a nondisclosure agreement, and

children.label: B
children.text: other Federal departments and agencies,

children.heading: Aggregated information
children.label: 3 Aggregated information
children.text: The Director may make aggregated, de-identified information available to contributing companies, Centers, or the public, as the Director considers appropriate, in support of the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Coordination with National Technology and Industrial Base Council
children.label: j Coordination with National Technology and Industrial Base Council
children.text: The Director, acting through the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership program, may work with the National Defense Technology and Industrial Base Council established under as the Director considers appropriate, to include in the National Supply Chain Database information regarding the defense manufacturing supply chain.section 4812 of title 10

children.label: A
children.text: shall be exempt from disclosure under ;section 552(b)(3) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: may not be made available pursuant to any Federal, State, local, or Tribal authority pursuant to any Federal, State, local, or Tribal law requiring public disclosure of information or records; and

children.label: C
children.text: may not, without the written consent of the private sector entity submitting such information, be used directly by the Director, or any other Federal, State, or local authority in any civil enforcement action brought by a Federal, State, Tribal, or local authority.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of written information or records, a written marking on the information or records substantially similar to the following: “This information is voluntarily submitted to the Federal Government in expectation of protection from disclosure as provided by the provisions of section 10253(k) of the Research and Development, Competition, and Innovation Act.”; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of oral information, a written statement similar to the statement described in subparagraph (A) submitted within a reasonable period following the oral communication.

children.heading: Private entities
children.label: 1 Private entities
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to require any private sector entity to share data, including proprietary information, with the Director or the National Supply Chain Database.

children.heading: Prohibition on new regulatory authority
children.label: 2 Prohibition on new regulatory authority
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to grant the Director, or the head of any other Federal agency, any authority to promulgate regulations or set standards on manufacturers, based on data within the National Supply Chain Database, that was not in effect on the day before .August 9, 2022
